english: tsig kún-tu zúg-pa (tshig kun tu zug pa) a sarcastic, offensive speech Stg. — 5. intrs., to bore or force itself into, to penetrate, to take hold, to stick to, mostly fig., e.g. sman ma zug (sman ma zug) the medicine has not taken hold yet, does not work Thgy.; zlá-la kyéd-kyis mi zug (zla la khyed kyis mi zug) you do not cling or stick to a companion Mil.; *dé-la sem zúg-pa* (*de la sems zug pa*) C. to be attached to, to be pleased with a thing; *zúg-pa* (*zug pa*) C., attached. — 6. to sting, like nettles, to prick, tser ltar (tsher ltar) like a thorn Mil.; ló-ma zúg-par byed (lo ma zug par byed) the leaves sting Wdṅ.; zug-rgyu-méd-pa (zug rgyu med pa) not smarting Wdṅ.

pf. btsud (btsud), Sch. also zud (zud), imp. tsud (tshud) (trs. to _tsúd-pa ('tshud pa), synon. to _júg-pa ('jug pa)), to put, to lay, into a box, into the grave; to lead, to guide, into the right way, to virtue, to religion = to convert; to reduce, to despair, sdig-pa-la (sdig pa la) to seduce to sin Pth.; to prompt one to do a thing Gyatch.; _dzúd-_dzud-pa ('dzud 'dzud pa) to put into Sch.

smile, byáms-pai _dzúm-yyis (byams pa'i 'dzum gyis) with a friendly smile; _dzum byéd-pa ('dzum byed pa) to smile; _dzum daṅ ldan ('dzum dang ldan) smiling Pth.; _dzum skyóṅ-ba ('dzum skyong ba) to preserve a friendly countenance, to be always mild and gentle; _dzum-skyoṅ ('dzum skyong) in a special sense, the exhortation given to every daughter on her marriage, to treat visitors with a friendly smile; also fig., an engaging appearance, ri-mo _dzúm-gyis ma bslús-par (ri mo 'dzum gyis ma bslus par) not to be deceived by an enticing appearance of colour Mil.; ṅo-_dzúm (ngo 'dzum), smile, in a relative sense, ă-nei ṅo-_dzúm dkar nag bltas (a ne'i ngo 'dzum dkar nag bltas) I watched whether the smile, the mien, of my aunt was friendly or unfriendly Mil.; ṅo-_dzúm nág-ste (ngo 'dzum nag ste) looking sad Dzl.

pf. btsum (btsum), zum (zum), fut. yzum (gzum), imp tsum (tsum) 1. to close, to shut, yet only in certain applications, more esp. to close one's eyes, to shut one's mouth, mig mi-_dzúm-par ltá-źiṅ (mig mi 'dzum par lta zhing) to have one's eyes immovably fixed upon Dzl.; also pád-mai ka zum bźin (pad ma'i kha zum bzhin) S.g. just as the lotus-flower closes; má-k'a mi zúm-źiṅ (rma kha mi zum zhing) Wdṅ. if the wound will not close; ka zum (kha zum) the orifice (of the urethra) is closed Mṅg. — 2. to wink, prob. only *dzum-dzúm jhé-pa* (*dzum dzum byed pa*) and *cò-ce* (*co ce*). — 3. to smile, ráb-tu (rab tu) to look very friendly Glr.; sbst. the smile, bcòm-ldan-_dás-kyi źal _dzúm-pa daṅ bcàs-pai sgó-nas (bcom ldan 'das kyi zhal 'dzum pa dang bcas pa'i sgo nas) from the portals of Buddha's countenance graced with a smile Glr.; źal-_dzúm mdzád-pa (zhal 'dzum mdzad pa) resp. to smile Glr.; bźin-gyi _dzum (bzhin gyi 'dzum) the smile of the countenance; adj. smiling; sweet, beautiful Mil. Comp. _dzúm-k'a ('dzum kha) a smiling mouth; lha-mo _dzúm-k'a-mo (lha mo 'dzum kha mo) a smiling goddess Mil. — _dzúm-bag-càn ('dzum bag can) (of a child) sweetly smiling Mil. — _dzum-ltag-dgyé ('dzum ltag dgye) Cs.: 'a smile between the teeth, a sardonic smile, a grin'; _dzum-mdáṅs ('dzum mdangs) a smiling air Cs. — _dzum-múl ('dzum mul) or -dmúl (-dmul) a smile; dzum-múl-gyis śor (dzum mul gyis shor) a smile escaped him Glr.; _dzum-(d)múl-ba ('dzum dmul ba) to smile. — _dzum-méd ('dzum med) frowning, austere Cs. — _dzum-_dzúm ('dzum 'dzum) 1. the winking. 2. the smiling; _dzum-waṅ-wáṅ ('dzum wang wang) Cs.: smiling look.
1. sup. of _dzu-ba ('dzu ba). 2. v. the following.

pf. bzur (bzur), fut. yzur (gzur), imp. zur (zur), Cs. *zúr-wa* (*zur wa*) to give or make way, lam(-nas) (lam nas) to step aside; to keep aloof Mil.; lás-la _dzúr-ba (las la 'dzur ba) to shun work, to evade labour Lex.
1. vb. to slip in, rtsa-yséb-tu (rtsa gseb tu) between the grass Thgy., sgor (sgor) through the door Lex.; čú-la (chu la), čur (chur) into the water, i.e. to dive. — 2. sbst. Sch.: 'a tippler'.
pf. _dzegs ('dzegs), imp. _dzog ('dzog), to ascend, rí-la (ri la) frq; śiṅ-sdoṅ-po-la (shing sdong po la) Glr.
to stick or jut out, to project, to be prominent Sch.

pf. bzed (bzed), fut. yzed (gzed), vulgo bzéd-pa (bzed pa), *zé-pa* (*ze pa*) C., *zéd-ce* (*zed ce*) W., to hold out or forth, kud (khud) the coat-tail, snod (snod) a vessel Dzl. (The significations given by Cs.: to receive, and by Sch.: to meet with, seem not to be sufficiently warranted.)
to shrink, la (la), from, to shun, avoid, mi-dgé-ba-la (mi dge ba la) Glr., sdig- (sdig)
